string.text:
A table showing how much damage this weapon does with each ammo type vs different bone/armor combinations. 
Assumes 20m range, 100% weapon condition, and doesn't account for attachments, artefacts, etc. 
 
%c[d_blue]Columns:
 %c[d_blue]Type:%c[default] Ammo type used for damage calculations on this row. 
%c[d_blue]AP:%c[default] Armor penetration colored grey to yellow by how well it punches through the listed armor values. 
%c[d_blue]vs Torso X:%c[default] Damage vs torso with X armor. Colored grey to green by how close it is to the max damage for this weapon+ammo combination. 
%c[d_blue]vs Head X:%c[default] Same as torso, but for headshots (inc 3.65x damage bonus). 
 
%c[d_blue]Notes:
 %c[default]- Numbers are first-shot estimates. If AP fails to penetrate, you get reduced chip damage instead of full damage. Once armor is worn down in a real fight you will do more - treat these mainly as your opening salvo. 
- BRC labels (10-60) are a player-friendly shorthand. Damage always uses hidden NPC bone-armor values underneath. 
- Not included: artefacts, ballistic plates, silencers, barrel wear, flat BR protection, or ranges other than 20 m. 
 
%c[d_blue]Math (20 m, current difficulty):
 %c[default]1. range_factor = 1 + 20/200 x (k_air x 0.5 / (1 - k_air + 0.1)) 
2. combat_AP = k_ap x 10 (+ 0.04 on head) x tier_ap_scale / range_factor x difficulty x 0.80 
3. power = hit_power / range_factor x ammo_k_hit x bone_mult x tier_ap_scale x 1.1 x difficulty x pellets 
 (bone_mult 0.9 torso / 3.65 head; pellets = buck_shot or 1) 
4. If combat_AP >= armor: damage = power x 100, else damage = power x chip_fraction x 100 
Difficulty: Scout/Stalker 1.30, Veteran 1.05, Master 0.90.

Chooses how armor tiers are labeled on the damage table. The damage math itself never changes - only the header text. 
 
%c[d_blue]Player BRC:%c[default] Friendly tier numbers (torso 10-60, head 10-45). Matches the BRC scale on player armor and ammo cards. 
%c[d_blue]NPC armor:%c[default] Real bone-armor values used in the damage formula (as percentages). 
 
%c[d_blue]Notes:
 %c[default]- Each column is still the same armor profile either way. BRC is just easier to read. 
 
%c[d_blue]Rough BRC matches in GAMMA:
 %c[default]Torso: 10 Novice, 20 Trainee, 30 Professional, 40 Veteran, 50 Master, 60 Legend. 
Head: 10 Respirator, 20 Screen helm, 30 Hardhat/ZSH, 35 Battle helm, 45 Exo/Spartan.

Adds two new stat rows, and appends real values after the existing GAMMA percentage stats. Assumes 100% weapon condition, default ammo, no attachments unless noted. Degraded ammo is skipped for damage rows. 
 
%c[d_blue]Hit Power:%c[default] New row. Weapon base hit_power per pellet as a percentage (x100). Ignores ammo and pellet count. 
%c[d_blue]Damage Per Ammo:%c[default] New row. Raw damage per ammo type in table order (e.g. 45/38/22). No range or armor. 
%c[d_blue]Accuracy:%c[default] Appended. ADS standing-still cone half-angle in degrees (e.g. 0.35 deg). Fixed per shot. 
%c[d_blue]Handling:%c[default] Appended. Hip-fire spread while walking and running (e.g. walk 2.1 deg / run 5.2 deg hip). 
%c[d_blue]Reliability:%c[default] Appended. Shots to lose 10% condition, best-to-worst ammo with silencer wear (e.g. 27-108). 
%c[d_blue]Recoil Control:%c[default] Appended. ADS camera kick: first-shot angle, then which shot reaches ~1 deg kick. 
%c[d_blue]Muzzle Velocity:%c[default] Appended. Bullet drop at 100 m from physics speed (e.g. -0.1m @100m). 
 
%c[d_blue]Notes / math:
 %c[default]- Hit Power = weapon hit_power x 100 (no ammo k_hit, no pellets). 
- Damage Per Ammo = hit_power x ammo_k_hit x pellets x 100. 
- Accuracy = fire_dispersion_base (degrees). 
- Handling = fire_dispersion_base scaled by actor PDM walk/run hip multipliers. 
- Reliability = floor(0.10 / (condition_shot_dec x ammo_impair)), worst end also x1.25 for silencer wear. 
- Recoil = zoom_cam_dispersion, then shot N where kick hits ~1 deg using zoom_cam_dispersion_inc. 
- Drop at 100 m = gravity x 100^2 / (2 x (bullet_speed x ammo_k_bullet_speed)^2).
Adds Hit Power and raw Damage Per Ammo stat rows, plus real values on Accuracy, Handling, Reliability, Recoil, and Muzzle Velocity.
